ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Precision Farming Specialists use data analytics and technology to optimize crop yields, reduce waste, and improve farming efficiency.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: They collect and analyze data from various sources, including sensors, drones, and satellite imagery, to identify areas of improvement and develop strategies for sustainable agriculture.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in agriculture and technology can pursue a degree in agriculture, computer science, or a related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue certifications in precision agriculture, data analytics, or a related field to bridge the gap.

future_builder.description: Experienced professionals in agriculture or data analytics can transition into precision farming by acquiring advanced skills in data science, machine learning, and programming.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2+ years of experience in agriculture or data analytics

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can switch to precision farming by acquiring relevant skills and building a portfolio of projects.
portfolio_proof_required: Project on crop yield prediction using machine learning

roadmap.title: Foundations in Agriculture and Data Science
